title: About Me
text:
Hi, I’m Marco Wang, a researcher and builder working at the intersection of speech technology, natural language processing, and multimodal AI. I am currently pursuing a Master of Data Science in Computational Linguistics at the University of British Columbia, where I focus on developing intelligent systems that can better understand and generate human language and speech.
My work is especially centered on speech and language technologies, including text-to-speech, speaker-related modeling, speech emotion recognition, and multimodal learning. I am interested in how different signals—such as audio, text, and speaker information—can be combined to create AI systems that are not only more accurate, but also more natural, adaptive, and useful in real-world settings.
Before coming to UBC, I worked as an undergraduate research assistant in speech and machine intelligence, where I gained hands-on experience building end-to-end machine learning pipelines for audio and language data. Since then, I have continued exploring research questions around speech processing, model alignment, and human-centered AI applications.
What excites me most is building AI that can make technology more accessible and meaningful in everyday life. I am particularly drawn to applications in areas like healthcare, communication, and assistive technology, where speech and language systems can have a direct positive impact on people’s lives.
Outside of research, I enjoy learning about new AI ideas, refining projects from concept to implementation, and thinking about how to turn technical advances into practical tools that people can actually use.


I’m always excited to connect with people who are interested in speech AI, multimodal learning, and building technology that helps people in meaningful ways.

title: Resume
text:

Machine learning engineer and data scientist with experience in computational linguistics, speech processing, and NLP systems, with hands-on experience on end-to-end ML pipelines, conversational AI applications, and multimodal speech systems.

title: Resume
text:
Work Experience
Graduate Research Assistant | Vancouver, BC
Speech, Language and Interactive Media Lab, The University of British Columbia | Aug 2025 – Present

Investigate how diffusion-based models improve text-to-speech by developing end-to-end experimentation workflows—from dataset curation and preprocessing to model training, inference, and performance reporting.

Research Assistant | Taipei, TW
Speech and Machine Intelligence Laboratory, National Taiwan Normal University | Nov 2024 – Jun 2025

Led a research project on multi-speaker Speech Emotion Recognition, improving alignment accuracy & data quality.
Developed a pipeline integrating WhisperX and Pyannote outputs into a novel alignment method, combining aligned audio segments with Wav2Vec 2.0 and RoBERTa via bimodal cross-attention fusion.
Achieved +10% accuracy improvement on the IEMOCAP dataset, demonstrating the benefits of using aligned, clean segments for SER in multi-speaker settings.

title: Resume
text:
Projects
Annotated Corpus Platform with Interactive Interface | Remote
Github | Mar 2026

Collaborated in a group of four to build an end-to-end annotated corpus system with a FastAPI-based interactive interface, supporting data annotation workflow and backend service for scalable data management.
Containerized the system with Docker for reproducibility and deployment, and are extending the platform to support model training and evaluation pipelines for under-resourced languages using the annotated corpus.

RAG-Based Recipe Chatbot | Remote
Github | Mar 2026

Developed a Python-based recipe chatbot using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings, and cosine-similarity vector search to answer natural-language queries over a cookbook dataset.
Integrated LLaMA 3.3 70B via NVIDIA API, intent classification, and recipe-scaling features to improve response relevance, functionality, and user experience.

Automated Pipeline for TTS Quality Assessment via Speaker Verification Analysis | Vancouver, BC
UBC | Dec 2025

Developed an automated evaluation pipeline for F5-TTS on 2,000+ LibriTTS audio segments, integrating SpeechBrain ECAPA-TDNN to extract speaker embeddings and quantify voice cloning quality using cosine similarity.
Built a scalable workflow for preprocessing, batch inference, and metric reporting, enabling efficient and reproducible evaluation of speaker preservation in zero-shot voice cloning.

Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) Project | Taipei, TW
NTNU | Sep 2024

Built an End-to-End ASR system using ESPnet2 with Transformer and Conformer models, achieving a 27.8% word error rate (WER) on the AN4 dataset using the Transformer model and compared it to Conformer.
Conducted comprehensive data analysis and optimized model parameters, resulting in a 15% performance improvement of the Transformer model over Conformer, and analyzed the stability and effectiveness in feature extraction and speech recognition tasks in both models.

title: Hsuan-Yu (Marco) Wang
text:
I am a student at the University of British Columbia (UBC) in the Master of Data Science in Computational Linguistics program, passionate about bridging the gap between computational linguistics and speech processing.


My primary interests lie in Data Science, Machine Learning, and Speech/Audio Modeling. I enjoy building tools that solve real-world problems—whether it’s uncovering underlying patterns behind phenomena or constructing fundamental end-to-end ASR/TTS pipelines.


Education
University of British Columbia | Vancouver, BC, Canada
